steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The parlor is called Tía Ana's 'special province' — where she runs her little school. Yet she has 'already come into the room several times' checking whether guests have left. The question asks why she keeps checking.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Tía Ana feels betrayed: the passage does not indicate betrayal; her checking is practical, not emotional in that way. ✗
  • Choice B — Tía Ana is joyful despite the room being 'never in order': the text does not support joy, and 'never in order' is a distraction unconnected to her motivation. ✗
  • Choice C — Tía Ana is seeking an escape: nothing in the passage suggests she wants to flee the situation. ✗
  • Choice D — the parlor is her school space, and Salomé's salon uses it every evening: the 'special province' detail directly explains her repeated checking — she needs the room back. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
